name: Computer Networks
description: Protocols, architectures and layers of computer networks.

questions.question: Which layer of the OSI model is responsible for providing reliable transport and delivery of data?
options: Network Layer
Data Link Layer
Transport Layer
Presentation Layer

questions.question: In packet switching, most switches use the 'store-and-forward' technique. What does this mean?
options: The router must reserve all resources along the path to its destination in advance.
The switch must receive the entire packet before it can start transmitting the first bit.
Data packets are necessarily held on a central server before being sent.
The link transmits the bits at the exact arrival rate, with no need to store them.

questions.question: What is the function of the Domain Name System (DNS)?
options: To map website names to their corresponding numeric IP addresses.
To allocate and configure core routers within web infrastructures.
To establish fully secure encryption barriers across the external network.
To guarantee fast routing for the flows coming from virtual applications.

questions.question: What is the conceptual definition of 'transmission delay' in a packet network?
options: Time to check for errors and determine the route.
Time the packet waits in the output queue.
Time to push all the bits onto the link.
Time for the signal to travel across the physical medium.

questions.question: Which of the characteristics below describes the UDP (User Datagram Protocol) protocol?
options: It guarantees minimum throughput levels and provides data security and encryption.
It requires a handshake before starting the transmission of data.
It performs unreliable data transfer between network processes.
It implements congestion control when the network is overloaded.

questions.question: What is the network topology in which a central device connects to all the other nodes?
options: Star
Ring
Bus
Mesh

questions.question: What is the cheapest and most widely used guided physical medium, which uses two twisted conductors to reduce electrical interference?
options: Coaxial cable
Twisted-pair copper wire
Optical fiber
Terrestrial radio channels

questions.question: The HTTP protocol is characterized as 'stateless'. What does this mean for the web server?
options: The server abruptly closes the TCP connection after sending packets.
The server sends data without applying any kind of encryption.
The server does not store the history or state of a client's requests.
The server natively prevents the use and storage of web cookies.

questions.question: In peer-to-peer (P2P) application architecture, how is communication organized?
options: End systems communicate directly with each other, without a permanent server.
All network nodes depend exclusively on a single dedicated central datacenter.
Exchanging information requires the mediation of specific edge routers.
Only one centralized device manages the distribution of data to the network.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the SMTP protocol in the email architecture?
options: To retrieve emails stored on the recipient's server in folders (as a pull protocol).
To deliver and store email messages on the recipient's server (as a push protocol).
To trace packet routes in order to map message delay across the network.
To convert the recipient's name into its corresponding IP address.
